patch.value:
## Evidence (2026-07-07)
pm next default (toon) output is 6123 bytes; the recommended block (id/title/type/status/priority/assignee/tags/updated_at/parent/open_blocker_count, ~450 bytes for pm-w89s) is repeated verbatim as ready[0] with every field identical. Same duplication in --json (8559 bytes). Every agent picking work pays the duplicated projection on every call.

## Why it matters
pm next is the highest-frequency read on the agent hot path (every work-pick loop). At thousands of concurrent agents (pm-9rxu scale story) the duplicated projection is pure waste, and it also trains agents to distrust the envelope (same item appears twice with no marker).

## Fix sketch
Omit the recommended item from ready (documented contract: ready = candidates EXCLUDING recommended), or replace ready[0] with a bare id reference (recommended_ref). Apply in one shared projection site so toon/json/MCP envelopes stay aligned; update SDK contracts + snapshot tests. Small-workspace neutral; strictly fewer tokens.
pm next repeats the recommended item verbatim as ready[0] — emit an id reference instead

Token-efficiency defect in the pm next envelope: the fully-projected recommended item is duplicated byte-identical as the first entry of the ready list, in both toon and json output. Duplicate check: searched next/duplicate/ready/envelope items — pm-gdi7 (flag hints, list-closed projection), pm-nilh (lean mutation default), pm-a9mc (validate counts-only) are adjacent but none covers the next envelope; no prior item found.

pm next (toon and json) no longer contains two full projections of the same item; the ready-list contract (excludes recommended, or id-ref) is documented in command docs and SDK contracts; contract snapshot tests assert non-duplication; MCP pm_next envelope matches

author: codex-root
message: Start broad atomic work-selection and agent recovery bundle; duplicate-safe all-status orientation complete

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Implementation start: active in the broad atomic work-selection and agent recovery PR. Live all-status duplicate check, full item metadata, closed prerequisite lineage, GitHub issues, security alerts, dependency audit, release state, and Sentry issue surface were reviewed before claiming. This item shares CLI/SDK contracts, recovery envelopes, and end-to-end verification with the seven sibling items in the bundle.

patch.value: Removed the recommended row from the ready tail while preserving evaluation and ranking semantics.
pm next emits one canonical recommended object without duplicating it as ready[0].
ready begins after the recommendation and SDK context evaluation treats recommended plus ready as the served ranking.

Live Sentry/telemetry: 0 blocking critical/high, 0.34% finish error rate, zero missing error-code rows. pm health: all 7 checks ok; history drift: 0 across 1,709 items.

patch.value: Implemented and verified in the atomic work-selection, dependency-integrity, recovery, alias, and telemetry bundle.
